concept: Exit / Go Out / Leave a Space
description: To go out of or leave a place. 'Thiru' names the outward crossing—moving from inside to outside, departure from enclosed space.
sound_symbolism: The word departs with awareness. 'Thi' begins with the focused breath of intentional movement, conscious of the threshold being crossed. 'Ru' moves with grounded purpose. Exit as mindful transition, not flight.
grammatical_notes: 'mia thiru' (I exit). Source takes 'mue' (out of): 'mia mue womu thiru.' (I exit the house.) The noun sense, the going out, comes by the event-noun rule. Opposite of 'koema' (enter).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Exiting is also beginning—leaving one space opens another, departure enables new arrival.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Exiting gracefully matters—how we leave shapes what we leave behind.
